claim_text: ding2020 is a representative deep-feature-distance / perceptual quality work.

sub_claims.text: Ding et al. 2020 proposes DISTS, a full-reference perceptual image quality assessment (IQA) method.

evidence.snippet: Here, we develop the first full-reference image quality model with explicit tolerance to texture resampling. ... We then describe an image quality method that combines correlations of these spatial averages ("texture similarity") with correlations of the feature maps ("structure similarity"). The parameters of the proposed measure are jointly optimized to match human ratings of image quality...

We show that the resulting Deep Image Structure and Texture Similarity (DISTS) index can be transformed into a proper metric in the mathematical sense. Moreover, DISTS correlates well with human quality judgments in several independent datasets...

Our goal is to develop a new full-reference IQA model that combines sensitivity to structural distortions (e.g., artifacts due to noise, blur, or compression) with a tolerance of texture resampling...

sub_claims.nuance: DISTS is explicitly framed as a perceptual IQA model trained against human image-quality ratings.

sub_claims.text: DISTS is built on deep CNN features (a modified VGG16 pretrained on ImageNet), making it a deep-feature-distance method.

evidence.snippet: More recently, deep CNNs have shown surprising power in representing perceptual image distortions [7], [8], [13]. In particular, Zhang et al. [7] have demonstrated that pre-trained deep features from VGG can be used as a substrate for quantifying perceptual quality.

As such, we also chose to base our model on the VGG16 CNN [14], pre-trained for object recognition [40] on the ImageNet database [41]. The VGG transformation is constructed by a feedforward cascade of layers, each including spatial convolution, halfwave rectification, and downsampling.
The representation then consists of the input image x, concatenated with the convolution responses of five VGG layers (labelled conv1 2, conv2 2, conv3 3, conv4 3, and conv5 3)
Our method is constructed by first nonlinearly transforming images to a multi-scale overcomplete representation, using a variant of the VGG convolutional neural network (CNN) [14].

sub_claims.nuance: DISTS sits in the same family as LPIPS (Zhang 2018) — both are VGG-feature-based perceptual distances, with DISTS adding texture-resampling tolerance.

phrasings_tried: DISTS deep image structure and texture similarity
structure similarity texture similarity full-reference quality

section_checklist.section: Applications to Texture Classification and Retrieval
Connections to Other Full-Reference IQA Methods

attestation.indirect_attribution_check: DISTS is a primary methods contribution of Ding et al. — not an attribution to another primary. The paper does build on prior IQA work (SSIM, LPIPS) but DISTS itself is original to this paper.
attestation.out_of_context_check: Manuscript appears to cite ding2020 as a representative example of deep-feature-distance / perceptual quality methods. The paper itself frames DISTS exactly as a deep-CNN-based perceptual IQA method, so usage matches source context.
